rulings.Case Title: CORNELL M. JANES et al VS. A. W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 4, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT ELLIOTT COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 16, 2008 PER EX PARTE STIPULATION AND ORDER ON MARCH 28, 2008. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: CORNELL M. JANES et al VS. A. W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 4, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT BUFFALO PUMPS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof in that defendant did not demonstrate that the pumps to which decedent was exposed failed to contain asbestos manufactured by defendant. Specifically, defendant offered no evidence on the distinction between original equipment and replacement parts as it relates to pumps. Assuming the burden shifted, plaintiff presented substantial evidence of decedent's exposure to defendant's pumps containing asbestos and plaintiff was not required to address the issue of whether this was original equipment or replacement parts supplied by defendant. Plaintiff also presented evidence of defendant's knowledge of the risks of exposure to asbestos. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action for negligence and for punitive damages for the same reasons the Court denied the motion for summary adjudication. Defendant did not shift the burden of proof on the 3rd, 4th causes of action because the special interrogatories do not address those causes of action. The objections to evidence will be addressed at the hearing. It does appear that this defendant had the opportunity to depose decedent for two days and that is sufficient to admit decedent's testimony as to this defendant. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CORNELL M. JANES et al VS. A. W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 4, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Crane shifted the burden of proof. Decedent's deposition testimony is not admissible as to this defendant. The Merrick Declaration shifts the burden of proof on the issue of original equipment. Plaintiff's evidence does not create a triable issue of fact becasue plaintiff has not presented evidence that decedent was exposed to asbestos that was original to the Crane product. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: CORNELL M. JANES et al VS. A. W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 4, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT IMO INDUSTRIES, INC.'S (INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO DELAVAL TURBINE, INC.) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF'S AND DECEDENT'S DISCOVERY RESPONSES IDENTIFIED DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT. BY FAILING TO SUBMIT SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH LIABILITY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: CORNELL M. JANES et al VS. A. W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 4, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT WARREN PUMPS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant shifted the burden of proof by relying on plaintiff's discovery responses which lack sufficient detail to establish that decedent was exposed to defendant's product containing asbestos. In opposition, plaintiff has presented evidence of exposure to defendant's pumps and that asbestos was present. Defendant claims that plaintiff failed to show that the asbestos was original equipment. However in moving for summary judgment, defendant did not raise that as an issue and plaintiff was required to do no more than present evidence of exposure to defendant's product that contained exposure. Defendant has failed to establish that its actions were at the direction of a federal officer or that the pumps at issue were manufactured at the specific direction of the government or that the government limited or precluded warnings. The motion for summary adjudication of the strict liability claim, the false representation claim, the intentional failure to warn are all based on the lack of exposure and plaintiff has demonstrated that there is a triable issue of fact on the issue of exposure. The motion for summary adjudication as to punitive damages is granted. Plaintiff's evidence consists solely of facts related to exposure and that is insufficient to provide a basis for punitive damages. Evidentiary issues will be addressed at the hearing. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 4, 2008 LINE 7. DEFENDANT HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: CLEVESTER WARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 4, 2008 LINE 7. DEFENDANT HAMILTON MATERIALS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF TESTIFIED THAT HE WORKED WITH ROOFING PRODUCTS AND DEFENDANT FAILED TO PRESENT COMPETENT EVIDENCE THAT IT DID NOT MAKE SUCH PRODUCTS. ASSUMING THE BURDEN WAS SHIFTED, PLAINTIFF PRESENTED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT BASED ON THE DECLARATIONS OF PLAINTIFF AND COHEN. PLAINTIFF MUST HAVE SIGNED HIS DECLARATION BY THE TIME OF THE HEARING. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED EXCEPT AS TO EXHIBIT C. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SUBMITTED A DECLARATION OF JOHN TEMPLIN AND THEREFORE, DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE WITHOUT MERIT. =(302/PJM/PB)
